steps.title: Compare the assessments
steps.content: Text 1 treats Orlando as an oddity and possibly minor because of its fantastical style and lighter tone. Text 2 accepts the lighter tone but argues that the novel seriously engages the same themes as Woolf's classic novels.

steps.title: Reject overstated responses
steps.content: Choice A says Woolf's talents were best suited to serious novels, which Text 2 does not concede. Choice B calls Orlando less impressive, but Text 2 argues for its importance. Choice D claims readers have overlooked it, which neither text establishes. The remaining choice acknowledges that Orlando differs from Woolf's other major novels while insisting on its centrality to her work. The answer is C.
